questions.question: What is geometry?
questions.correct_answer: A branch of mathematics that deals with shapes, sizes and properties of space
questions.explanation: The word geometry comes from Greek words meaning 'earth measurement'.

questions.question: Who was Euclid?
questions.correct_answer: A Greek mathematician who organised geometry in a systematic way
questions.explanation: Euclid compiled the famous mathematical work called 'Elements'.

questions.question: What are undefined terms in geometry?
questions.correct_answer: Basic terms such as point, line and plane that are not formally defined
questions.explanation: They are understood intuitively and used to build other concepts.

questions.question: What is an axiom?
questions.correct_answer: A statement accepted as true without proof
questions.explanation: Axioms are universal truths used throughout mathematics.

questions.question: What is a postulate?
questions.correct_answer: An assumption specific to geometry that is accepted without proof

questions.question: State Euclid’s first postulate.
questions.correct_answer: A straight line may be drawn from any one point to any other point
questions.explanation: It states that a straight line can connect any two points.

questions.question: What does Euclid’s second postulate state?
questions.correct_answer: A terminated line can be produced indefinitely
questions.explanation: A line segment can be extended endlessly in both directions.

questions.question: What does Euclid’s third postulate state?
questions.correct_answer: A circle can be drawn with any centre and any radius
questions.explanation: This postulate forms the basis of circle construction.

questions.question: What does Euclid’s fourth postulate state?
questions.correct_answer: All right angles are equal to one another
questions.explanation: Any right angle has the same measure of 90 degrees.

questions.question: What is a theorem?
questions.correct_answer: A mathematical statement that is proved using axioms, postulates and definitions
questions.explanation: Theorems are derived through logical reasoning.

notes.title: Undefined Terms
notes.content: Point, line and plane are basic undefined terms in geometry.

notes.title: Euclid’s Elements
notes.content: Euclid compiled geometric knowledge into thirteen books called Elements.

notes.title: Axioms
notes.content: Axioms are universal truths accepted without proof.

notes.title: Postulates
notes.content: Postulates are assumptions that apply specifically to geometry.

notes.title: Theorem
notes.content: A theorem is a statement proved logically using axioms, definitions and postulates.

facts.fact: Geometry originated from the need to measure land.
Euclid organised geometric knowledge into a logical system.
Axioms and postulates are accepted without proof.
Two distinct lines cannot intersect at more than one point.
